records.Activity Name: BioLab Sales and Innovation Lab Gift and Sponsorship
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Public Service (PS) - activity is primarily ASU-led education, training, or public programming.
records.Activity Description (Short): BioLab Holdings gave us a $2.2M gift to build our BioLab Sales and Innovation Lab in the Department of Marketing. This will be a physical lab where we can promote experiential learning for our professional sales students. The gift also includes scholarships, travel funding for students, and a high school outreach program. They also gave $150K gift that includes a 3-year sponsorship and money for a Biosciences case competition. Our main contact, Jaime Leija, recently won the Founder's Day Alumni Achievement award. He is highly engaged with our program, mentoring students and speaking in classes and with our student organizations.

records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): In FY26, ASU and Amkor Technology Arizona achieved the most significant milestone in the partnership to date by finalizing a CHIPS Act intermediary agreement. The agreement positions ASU as the workforce intermediary supporting Amkor’s $7 million workforce development strategy, including coordination of strategic partners, workforce solutions, compliance, reporting, and talent pipeline activities across K-12, CTE, community colleges, universities, veterans, and transitioning service members. This is important because it gives ASU a formal and funded role in Arizona’s semiconductor workforce ecosystem, strengthens student and worker pathways into high-demand advanced manufacturing careers, and creates a long-term platform for deeper partnership with Amkor around workforce, academic programs, and regional economic development.

records.Activity Name: Data Centers and Artificial Intelligence – Arizona Knowledge Exchange and Strategy Initiative
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - partner is involved in research, capstone, or collaborative project work.
records.Activity Description (Short): Project: Data Centers and Artificial Intelligence – Arizona Knowledge Exchange and Strategy Initiative Arizona State University, through the Julie Ann Wrigley Global Futures Laboratory and Energy Forward, is leading a multi-stakeholder initiative to better understand and shape the rapid expansion of data centers and AI infrastructure in Arizona. The effort brings together industry, government, academia, and community stakeholders to address economic, environmental, and policy implications of large-scale digital infrastructure growth. Flagship Project A coordinated initiative to develop a shared, fact-based understanding of data center development in Arizona, examining impacts across energy, water, land use, and community systems while identifying pathways for balanced, sustainable growth. Delivery Model - Multi-sector convening model integrating academia, industry, utilities, policymakers, and community stakeholders - Knowledge exchange events, including the February 11 convening - Multidisciplinary working group composed of ASU experts and industry leaders - Ongoing event series and topic-specific discussions (in development) - Development of practical playbooks for industry, municipalities, and communities FY26 Partnership Accomplishments & Impact - Convened a standing-room-only knowledge exchange event with over 270 attendees from industry, academia, government, and community organizations - Established a neutral forum to examine both benefits and challenges of data center growth, including economic development, infrastructure demand, and community impacts - Advanced understanding of key issues including energy demand, water use, siting considerations, and urban heat impacts, with early research indicating potential localized temperature increases near facilities - Facilitated dialogue among utilities, developers, policymakers, and researchers to better align on infrastructure planning and growth management - Initiated a multidisciplinary working group to further evaluate impacts and identify solutions - Established a foundation for continued engagement and applied guidance development Strategic Value to ASU - Positions ASU as a neutral convener and thought leader on AI infrastructure and data center development - Strengthens ASU’s role at the intersection of energy, water, technology, and policy - Establishes a platform for ongoing engagement with industry and decision-makers - Expands ASU’s leadership in translating research into actionable strategies Core Team: - Gary Dirks - Kelly Barr - Global Futures Laboratory - Energy Forward - NSF Futures Engine

records.Activity Name: U.S. Army Regional IGSA Energy Services Partnership
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - formal partnership, MOU, LOI, or agreement suggests shared goals.
records.Activity Description (Short): Project: Regional Intergovernmental Support Agreement (IGSA) Program – U.S. Army IMCOM (Pentagon-Level) Arizona State University is the first university in the nation to execute a Regional IGSA (R-IGSA) for Energy Services with the U.S. Army, establishing a new federal partnership model for delivering energy, infrastructure, and resilience solutions at scale to all Army installations across the continental US (CONUS). Flagship Project: The School for Engineering of Matter, Transport, and Energy (SEMTE) at ASU - through the framework of the ITAC program, serves as the primary execution partner for the IGSA program. ASU LightWorks - working in partnership with Ft Huachuca and ITAC - identified, convened, and advanced the foundational program and partnerships that enabled this first-of-its-kind 10-year agreement with the U.S. Army. Delivery Model: • ASU LightWorks serves as the Operations Coordination Hub • Centralized governance with distributed execution across ASU units and partners • Task-order-based deployment model enabling scalable, multi-site implementation FY26 Partnership Accomplishments & Impact: • Executed 10-year IGSA agreement with U.S. Army IMCOM • Established ASU-led governance and delivery framework for energy services • Activated initial project pipeline at Ft. Huachuca (flagship installation) • Expanded engagement across Arizona installations: Yuma Proving Ground, Luke AFB • Developed a replicable national model for energy resilience, infrastructure modernization, and workforce alignment Strategic Value to ASU: • Positions ASU as a national leader in DoD energy and infrastructure partnerships • Establishes a scalable platform for federally funded task orders and programs • Integrates research, operations, and workforce development into a unified delivery model • Strengthens ASU’s university-to-installation engagement pipeline across CONUS
records.ASU Faculty/Staff Involved: Kelly Barr (Associate Vice President and Chief Alliance Officer) - Kelly.Barr@asu.edu; Additional ASU collaborators from survey: • Ryan Klenner – Operational Lead (LightWorks) • Ryan Milcarek – Technical Lead (ITAC) • ASU Industrial Training and Assessment Center (ITAC) – Technical implementation and energy assessment • LightWorks / Global Futures Laboratory – Program strategy, coordination, and delivery • Cochise College - on the ground students to support on-site activities and task orders at Ft. Huachuca

records.Activity Name: LG AI Hatchery Living Innovation Lab Concept
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - formal partnership, MOU, LOI, or agreement suggests shared goals.
records.Activity Description (Short): Strategic Objectives: § Establish a technology standard based on use case, space, and application- the Pub, the Dean's Suite, Classrooms/Ideation Spaces, the War Room, the Main Entrance, 1st Floor (Forum, Hatchery, Lab) and the Museum Concept - to simplify design and procurement. § The AI Hatchery & Venture Incubator: LG should collaborate with Thunderbird and their AI Hatchery concept to provide a 'living lab' that anchors the first-floor innovation ecosystem - integrating the Language Lab (AI-mediated language learning), Computer Lab, and 3D Printing/Maker Space. This incubator will serve as a premier destination for fellowship programs where students explore AI Diplomacy and business applications. Crucially, we will co-develop a technology-forward environment that is attractive for recruiting external AI businesses to invest and consult. As part of this commitment, LG can facilitate high-level introductions organizations within LG, such as LG Ventures, creating a direct pipeline between student-led innovation and corporate venture capital. § Consultative Co-Creation of the Global Trade Laboratory: LG should serve as close consultants to align specific display and use-case requirements with the lab’s design. Our role is to identify where LG can support the build-out and which ecosystem partners we should engage to help them realize this world-class, immersive laboratory. § Exploring Institutional & Global Talent Alignment: We will explore opportunities to move beyond a traditional hardware relationship by evaluating how LG might engage with Thunderbird’s Executive Education and leadership programs. This includes discussing potential pathways for global management development - perhaps involving collaborations with South Korean universities - and investigating how LG might support the student pipeline through internships, capstone project consideration, or workforce development initiatives tied to local LG facilities. § Scaling the Innovation Corridor: LG should establish a collaborative pipeline focused on VOC for future technologies, POC testing, and pilot opportunities, starting within the Thunderbird ecosystem and expanding to ASU at large. This corridor will drive equity of access to education and global learning outcomes while directly supporting the student experience through on-campus events, including guest speaker series, hackathons, and technology learning days. ______________________ Global Trade Laboratory - Consult on existing architectural vision to align display specs with desired use cases. The AI Hatchery & Incubator - Build an attractive environment for AI business partners; includes Language Lab, Computer Lab, and 3D printing suites. Minimalist Storytelling (Museum) - 1st-floor 'Museum Concept' anchor point; app-based tours for alumni; subtle Transparent OLED layers on 5th floor. ASU MIX VP Remediation - Address competitive LED failure in Virtual Production studio due to haze/moisture using LG’s ruggedized/weather-resistant LED solutions. Innovation Corridor (VOC/POC) - Pipeline for VOC, pilot opportunities, and campus events (Hackathons/Tech Days) starting at T-Bird and scaling to ASU. Exec Ed & Talent Exploration - Evaluate opportunities for LG leadership dev, potential South Korean university alignment, and local workforce development. Venture Pipeline (Exploratory) - Facilitate high-level introductions between student ventures and the incubator to LG Ventures.

records.Activity Name: Mayo Clinic Reciprocal Research Access Collaboration
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): ASU Core Research Facilities and Mayo Clinic Research Core Facilities launched a strategic collaboration that gives researchers at both institutions reciprocal access to each other’s core facilities and technologies at internal pricing rates, removing a major financial barrier to collaboration. The partnership combines ASU’s strengths in interdisciplinary research and advanced technology with Mayo Clinic’s clinical and translational expertise to accelerate scientific discovery and improve patient outcomes. The collaboration also establishes a shared research support framework that includes joint training, technical expertise and grant development resources to strengthen large-scale biomedical research initiatives.

records.Activity Name: Starbucks College Achievement Plan
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): Starbucks College Achievement Plan (SCAP) A first-of-its-kind education program, SCAP continues to demonstrate significant impact through enrollment growth, degree completion, and expanded access to higher education for Starbucks partners. In May 2026, the program celebrated its largest graduating class to date with more than 1,100 partners earning their ASU degree. -Nearly 20,000 graduates to date -27,000+ participating partners -6,000+ scholars admitted through Pathway to Admission -20%+ of participants are first-generation college students -Nearly 90% of U.S. stores have at least one partner in the program Starbucks Global Academy (SGA) SGA continues to expand its global reach and learner engagement, surpassing 1 million course enrollments while delivering accessible learning opportunities to individuals around the world. -356,000 unique learners -1.436M course enrollments -764,000 course completions This year, SGA launched a new learning collection, Craft of Leadership, featuring authentic stories and leadership lessons from Starbucks leaders designed to encourage ownership, simplicity, customer focus, and collaboration. Hacienda Alsacia & Starbucks Innovation Farm Through collaboration connected to Hacienda Alsacia, Starbucks and ASU continue advancing coffee innovation, sustainability research, and experiential learning opportunities for ASU students and SCAP scholars: SOS 394: The Future of Coffee Launched in Fall 2025 through the School of Sustainability, this course explores how climate change, urbanization, biodiversity loss, and policy shifts are reshaping the global coffee supply chain. The course will be offered again in Fall 2026 and is taught by Professor Halle Eakin. SOS 494: Global Coffee and Sustainability in Costa Rica Fifteen SCAP scholars participated in the third offering of this Winter Break study abroad experience, which combines sustainability education, cultural immersion, and hands-on learning at Hacienda Alsacia. Due to strong interest and continued support from Starbucks, the program is expected to expand to ~30 scholars in December 2026. This program is led by Dave White, Director of the Global Institute of Sustainability and Innovation (GIOSI), and Lauren Bataska, Program Manager GIOSI. Graduate Research Assistantship Starbucks is funding a graduate research assistant within the School of Sustainability under the supervision of Professor Dave White to support coffee innovation research and explore emerging ideas, technologies, and insights that can help shape the future of coffee. Executive Leadership Engagement Since joining Starbucks as CEO in September 2024, Brian Niccol has prioritized attending SCAP graduation ceremonies at ASU. Starbucks executive leadership team has also deepened engagement with ASU through campus visits, participation in SCAP celebrations, and collaboration on future-focused partnership opportunities. ASU Board Participation Erin Haggard, Vice President of Coffeehouse Excellence, joined the Center for Services Leadership Board for 2026–2027. In her role, Erin leads customer service, deployment, channel operations, partner experience, and operational standards across the coffeehouse business. Updates to Bespoke Course Creation FSE 302: Innovation in Corporate Technology Based on feedback related to the robustness of curricular material, the ASU team reimagined this course and delivered it as a 15-week offering for the first time in Spring 2026 to over 50 students, including SCAP scholars. An official course number, outside of the generic “FSE 394, Special Topics” was also secured, recognizing the longevity of this curriculum and the value it provides to Fulton students. SCM 394: Supply Chain Management of the Coffee Industry ASU and Starbucks collaboratively reimagined ASU’s Supply Chain Management of the Coffee Industry course in Spring 2026, resulting in doubled enrollment and expanded scholar engagement. As part of the experience, 19 SCAP scholars visited the Starbucks Support Center in Seattle and participated in immersive learning opportunities, including the Port of Seattle, where they explored the intersection of logistics, trade, infrastructure, and the global coffee supply chain firsthand. This credit-bearing study away experience supports scholars interested in supply chain careers by combining applied learning, industry engagement, and case study development with direct exposure to Starbucks leaders and operations.

This includes, but is not limited to: EdPlus, University Affairs, Media Relations and Strategic Communication, ASU Brand, ASU Foundation, College of Global Futures, School of Sustainability, W. P. Carey School of Business, The College of Integrative Arts and Sciences, and Fulton Schools of Engineering. records.Activity Name: Fulton Schools of Engineering OSHA Training
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): In FY26 (July 2025–June 2026), the partnership between Arizona State University, the ASU Foundation, and the Blackstone Charitable Foundation advanced significantly through workforce development, entrepreneurship, and career-readiness initiatives that expanded access to experiential learning and skilled trades pathways for Arizona students. A major accomplishment during FY26 was the successful implementation and expansion of the Blackstone LaunchPad Summer Internship Pilot. Through Blackstone Charitable Foundation’s $180,000 investment, ASU and the Maricopa County Community College District (MCCCD) provided 30 paid internships to students across 15 Phoenix-area companies. The program delivered hands-on professional experiences in areas including software, engineering, digital marketing, robotics, venture capital analysis, nonprofit services, and entrepreneurship. The initiative generated extraordinary student demand, receiving 1,911 applications for the 30 internship positions, demonstrating both the need for paid experiential learning opportunities and the strength of the Blackstone LaunchPad brand. The internship initiative also produced measurable outcomes for students and employer partners. All participating students successfully completed their internships, while 100% of participating companies indicated they would recommend hiring ASU and MCCCD student interns in the future. Additionally, 62% of participating companies reported they were considering extending full-time or part-time offers to their interns. The program strengthened ASU’s relationships with Phoenix-area employers and enhanced the visibility and reputation of ASU, MCCCD, and the Blackstone LaunchPad program as a trusted pipeline for entrepreneurial and career-ready talent. Building on this success, FY26 also included the development of a large-scale collaborative proposal focused on strengthening Arizona’s skilled trades workforce pipeline. ASU, the Center for the Future of Arizona (CFA), MCCCD, the Fulton Schools of Engineering OSHA Training Institute, the Greater Phoenix Chamber Foundation, and the Phoenix Future Talent Fund aligned around a coordinated strategy to address Arizona’s growing labor shortages in construction, electrical work, HVAC, and advanced manufacturing. The initiative proposed a $3 million investment to expand recruitment, scholarships, stipends, training capacity, and employer engagement across Arizona. The partnership established ambitious statewide impact goals, including supporting more than 5,000 individuals through training, upskilling, and employment pathways, engaging nearly 200 employers, and increasing access to high-wage, high-demand careers through scholarships, apprenticeships, work-based learning, and workforce-aligned curriculum development. Additional FY26 accomplishments included: • Strengthening ASU’s collaboration with MCCCD and regional workforce partners to align educational pathways with industry demand. • Expanding employer engagement with companies such as McCarthy Building Companies, Mortenson Construction, Lowe’s, Goettl Air Conditioning, DPR Construction, and IBEW Local 640. • Advancing plans for scholarships and stipends to reduce barriers related to tuition, transportation, childcare, equipment, and certification costs for students pursuing skilled trades careers. • Developing strategies to increase instructional capacity, training facilities, and industry-aligned curriculum across multiple community college campuses to meet Arizona’s workforce demands.
records.ASU Faculty/Staff Involved: Jennie Denison (Director Global Corporate Advancement) - jennie.denison@asufoundation.org; Additional ASU collaborators from survey: This partnership has been highly collaborative across multiple ASU units and support functions. Key collaborators include Academic Alliances within the Office of the University Provost, which serves as a central lead for transfer pathways and workforce alignment initiatives, as well as the Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering and the OSHA Training Institute Education Center (OSHA Center), which support workforce training, safety certification programming, and skilled trades curriculum development. Additional collaboration has included the Entrepreneurship + Innovation Institute (Edson E+I), which led implementation of the Blackstone LaunchPad Summer Internship Pilot and coordinated employer engagement, student recruitment, internship placements, and career-readiness programming. The partnership also required significant coordination with institutional support and operational teams across ASU and the ASU Foundation, including: • ASUEP Media Relations • ASUF Writing Services • Gift Agreements • ASUF General Counsel • ASU General Counsel • ORSPA (Office of Research Sponsored Projects Administration) These teams supported proposal development, communications, legal review, agreement execution, sponsorship and gift structuring, compliance, and institutional coordination necessary to advance the partnership successfully.

records.Activity Name: Living Innovation Lab
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): In FY26, the partnership between Arizona State University and GoDaddy made substantial progress in establishing a first-of-its-kind model focused on student-athlete entrepreneurship, digital brand development, and innovation. Through GoDaddy’s Empower initiative and ASU’s SPORTx and J. Orin Edson Entrepreneurship + Innovation Institute, ASU scaled the year-round program providing student-athletes with mentorship, entrepreneurial education, co-working space, funding opportunities, and access to GoDaddy’s digital tools and AI-powered website resources. The partnership also evolved into a broader strategic vision centered on a “Living Innovation Lab” designed to scale entrepreneurship and digital empowerment initiatives across ASU, the Big 12, and the NCAA ecosystem. Together, ASU and GoDaddy engaged hundreds of student-athletes and entrepreneurs through workshops, venture-building curriculum, NIL-focused programming, and digital branding support, while creating scalable playbooks and toolkits intended for national replication. Building on the success of the pilot phase, GoDaddy committed a transformative $3,000,000 multi-year gift investment to expand student entrepreneurship pitch competitions, operational programming, and the SPORTx Entrepreneurship Cup — a national entrepreneurship competition and showcase for student-athletes. The funding supports Venture Devils competitions, athlete venture funding, travel stipends, operational support, and national-scale programming intended to position ASU and GoDaddy as leaders in athlete entrepreneurship and innovation across higher education.
records.ASU Faculty/Staff Involved: Jennie Denison (Director Global Corporate Advancement) - jennie.denison@asufoundation.org; Additional ASU collaborators from survey: This partnership has been deeply collaborative across multiple ASU and ASU Foundation teams, reflecting the cross-functional nature and strategic scale of the initiative. Key collaborators include SPORTx at ASU and the J. Orin Edson Entrepreneurship + Innovation Institute, which have led program development, athlete engagement, entrepreneurship programming, and national expansion strategy. The ASU Office of the COO has played an important leadership and alignment role in advancing the partnership vision and institutional coordination. Additional collaboration has included ASUF Writing Services for proposal and communications support; ASUF General Counsel and ASU General Counsel for legal review, intellectual property, NIL, and compliance considerations; ASUF Gift Agreements for structuring and execution of philanthropic support; Outreach Hub for communications and visibility strategy; and ASUF Donor Relations for stewardship, reporting, and donor engagement. Together, these teams have helped position the partnership as a scalable, enterprise-level model for student entrepreneurship, athlete innovation, and university-industry collaboration.

records.Students Outside Coursework?: Yes/likely - student engagement outside coursework is described or implied by the source narrative. Source basis: In FY26, the partnership between Arizona State University and GoDaddy made substantial progress in establishing a first-of-its-kind model focused on student-athlete entrepreneurship, digital brand development, and innovation. Through GoDaddy’s Empower initiative and ASU’s SPORTx and J. Orin Edson Entrepreneurship + Innovation Institute, ASU scaled the year-round program providing student-athletes with mentorship, entrepreneurial education, co-working space, funding opportunities, and access to GoDaddy’s digital tools and AI-powered website resources. The partnership also evolved into a broader strategic vision centered on a “Living Innovation Lab” designed to scale entrepreneurship and digital empowerment initiatives across ASU, the Big 12, and the NCAA ecosystem. Together, ASU and GoDaddy engaged hundreds of student-athletes and entrepreneurs through workshops, venture-building curriculum, NIL-focused programming, and digital branding support, while creating scalable playbooks and toolkits intended for national replication. Building on the success of the pilot phase, GoDaddy committed a transformative $3,000,000 multi-year gift investment to expand student entrepreneurship pitch competitions, operational programming, and the SPORTx Entrepreneurship Cup — a national entrepreneurship competition and showcase for student-athletes. The funding supports Venture Devils competitions, athlete venture funding, travel stipends, operational support, and national-scale programming intended to position ASU and GoDaddy as leaders in athlete entrepreneurship and innovation across higher education.

records.Activity Name: Semiconductor Education and Workforce Initiatives in Indonesia
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - partner is involved in research, capstone, or collaborative project work.

records.Activity Name: Macquarie Capital AI Professional Development and Applied Fellowship
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): Macquarie Capital has expressed interest in ASU as a long-term institutional partner across education, workforce development, and innovation. Pending successful initial pilots, Macquarie Capital envisions ASU as its global education and research innovation partner. Skills for the Future at Macquarie Capital is a professional development and workforce training proposal for Macquarie Capital employees, led by ASU’s Learning Enterprise and WP Carey School of Business. Macquarie Capital kicked off a 6-week sprint with Accenture on April 27th 2026, examining AI competency and exploring AI workflow integration. A 'workbench for junior bankers' is being built with Accenture. There are opportunities for collaboration between ASU Learning Enterprise and WP Carey on a professional development curriculum aligned to the Accenture/Macquarie sprint findings, as well as an opportunity for ASU to design and deliver a 2-day AI professional development course for Macquarie Capital's 50+ employee cohort. Longer-term there is an opportunity to build and deliver an in-person AI module for senior Macquarie Capital leaders. Macquarie-ASU Applied Fellowship Program: is a proposal still in the early scoping phases. The program is designed to embed ASU graduate and undergrad students in Macquarie project teams to conduct applied research aligned to Macquarie's investment theses, with pathways to employment. The Fellowship would be semester-based (6-month) co-op format. There is an agreement to move forward with a WP Carey co-op program for undergrad Jrs. and Srs. in Spring 2027 with future potential to expand to graduate fellowships in engineering and business. Macquarie - ASU Venture Studio: The final proposal for a joint venture studio is pending approval from Macquarie Capital. This would become a hybrid academia-industry venture creation engine — ASU as national testbed, Macquarie as capital and innovation partner — building next-gen companies at the intersection of advanced technology, AI, engineering, and the New Economy.

records.Activity Name: Applied Materials Research Funding and Materials-to-Fab Center
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): ASU’s collaboration with Applied Materials expanded significantly this year, with research funding surpassing $10 million. This investment supported more than 40 workshops, generated over 50 research proposals, and resulted in 45 approved projects involving more than 50 graduate students across multiple disciplines. A major milestone in the partnership was the grand opening of Applied Materials’ Materials-to-Fab (MTF) Research Center at ASU’s Macro Technology Works (MTW). The event, held during the inaugural SEMICON West in Phoenix, featured Arizona Governor Katie Hobbs, ASU President Michael Crow, and Applied Materials President Prabu Raja, underscoring the strategic importance of the collaboration to Arizona’s growing semiconductor ecosystem. Applied Materials has become the largest and most strategic tenant at MTW, further deepening its engagement with ASU through the launch of its inaugural internship program for students participating in joint research projects. In Summer 2026, the program welcomed its first cohort of 10 ASU student interns, providing hands-on industry experience and strengthening the talent pipeline between the university and the semiconductor industry. Building on this momentum, Applied Materials also selected ASU as a partner in its strategic EPIC Center initiative, designed to accelerate U.S. lab-to-fab innovation by integrating academic research with industry-leading equipment, manufacturing processes, and commercialization expertise.

records.Activity Name: NSF Futures Engine Regional Innovation and P-20 Education Initiative
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - formal partnership, MOU, LOI, or agreement suggests shared goals.
records.Activity Description (Short): The NSF Futures Engine completed its second year and achieved significant accomplishments including: -188 distinct organizations joined as partners, coming from the Southwest US and beyond, bringing capabilities in research, regional economic development, venture capital, workforce development, local governments, and P-20 education. -Innovation Cohort of 8 startups supported to advance TRL to commercialization stage with funding, mentoring, networking, and acceleration services. -Developed a regional strategic plan to advance water, energy, and air innovations needed across the Southwest US. -Implemented an events process for community perspectives to be shared across the region and sectors, e.g. Data Center and AI Infrastructure Knowledge Exchange, International Atmospheric Water Harvesting Summit. -Completed 16 catalyst grant projects at anchor university partners to advance bench research to pilot phase. -Career and Skill Paths for energy, water, and air employment opportunities across the Southwest US. -Supported and increased the number of testbeds across the region in energy, water, and air for startups and innovations to advance towards commercialization.

records.Activity Description (Short): ASU and TSMC executed five new joint development projects between ASU faculty and TSMC technical owners, totaling $600,000. records.Activity Description (Short): Blue Zones Health (BZH) and ASU partnered to develop and deliver a foundational learning experience designed to increase awareness and understanding of Blue Zones principles among clinicians and affiliated healthcare staff. The program provides participants with practical insights into the lifestyle, environmental, and community factors that contribute to improved health outcomes and longevity. Now in its second year of delivery, the program continues to support BZH’s efforts to educate and engage healthcare professionals around preventive health and well-being. To date, the program has served 150+ learners across the BZH network, helping expand access to evidence-based concepts that promote healthier communities and improved quality of life.

records.Activity Name: State of Arizona Public-Sector Workforce Development Initiative
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): ASU and the Arizona Department of Administration launched a statewide professional development initiative supported by $1 million in state funding to strengthen Arizona’s public-sector workforce. The pilot generated more than 1,300 applications across 60 state agencies, enrolling over 700 employees in programs focused on leadership, data analytics, project management, AI, and workplace effectiveness. Early outcomes show strong impact, with 99% of learners who completed their coursework reporting increased confidence in applying new skills and 86% indicating the experience strengthened their ability to serve Arizona. To expand access, ASU also launched a statewide keynote series that engaged more than 2,200 employees through faculty-led online sessions on AI, leadership, communication, and data-informed decision-making. The initiative established a scalable model for workforce development that advances employee capability, engagement, and public service outcomes across Arizona state government.

records.Activity Name: Aramark Sustainability Learning Experiences
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - partner provides funding or sponsorship tied to shared activity outcomes.
records.Activity Description (Short): ASU completed the development of two custom sustainability learning experiences for Aramark, designed to embed sustainability education into employee development and customer engagement at scale. The programs represent a strategic investment in innovative learning solutions that bring Aramark’s sustainability commitments to life through practical, accessible experiences. The first program, Reknowable Resources, uses QR-code-enabled, mobile-friendly microlearning experiences that connect learners directly to the sustainability impact of products, practices, and operations in real-world settings. The second, Be Well. Do Well. In Action., is a highly interactive and gamified learning journey that helps employees explore how Aramark’s sustainability platform is implemented across business lines and customer experiences. Both programs successfully completed user testing in FY26 and are scheduled for enterprise launch in Q1 FY27, positioning Aramark to expand sustainability awareness and engagement across its workforce and customer ecosystem.

records.Activity Name: PepsiCo Water Security and Stewardship Program

records.Activity Description (Short): PepsiCo and ASU continued their collaboration to advance global water stewardship education through the Water Security and Stewardship program, originally developed in partnership with The Nature Conservancy. Hosted on Coursera, the program equips professionals with practical strategies for addressing water scarcity, sustainability, and responsible resource management, and has reached more than 2,200 learners worldwide. The partnership also expanded beyond online learning this year, with a PepsiCo team member participating in ASU’s Conservation Futures Academy. This engagement created new opportunities for collaboration around sustainability leadership, conservation innovation, and workforce development, strengthening the strategic relationship between PepsiCo and ASU.

records.Activity Name: Regenerative Grazing Open-Air Lab
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - partner provides funding or sponsorship tied to shared activity outcomes.
records.Activity Description (Short): Cargill provided catalytic funding to support the development of an innovative workforce development initiative designed to expand climate-smart agriculture practices while creating new career pathways for transitioning service members. In partnership with the Naval Postgraduate School’s Regenerative Grazing Open-Air Lab (R-GOAL), ASU is developing a digital microcredential that prepares learners to implement regenerative grazing and adaptive land management practices that improve soil health, strengthen water resilience, and reduce wildfire risk. The initiative combines ASU’s expertise in scalable workforce learning with R-GOAL’s field-tested regenerative grazing model to create a structured pathway from military service to careers in regenerative agriculture. With funding secured and development underway, the program is positioned to pilot its first cohort in early FY27 and establish a scalable workforce pipeline supporting climate resilience, conservation, and sustainable land management nationwide.

records.Activity Name: Uber and ASU Continued to Expand Access to Workforce and Economic Mobility
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - partner provides funding or sponsorship tied to shared activity outcomes.
records.Activity Description (Short): Uber and ASU continued to expand access to workforce and economic mobility education through the Uber Pro partnership, providing eligible earners with access to ASU’s non-degree learning portfolio in entrepreneurship and English language development. To date, the programs have engaged over 11,000 unique learners and generated more than 20,000 course enrollments. Learner feedback remains strong. In ASU’s Learn English Now program, 97% of participants reported that the course met their expectations and 93% found the content highly engaging. In the Entrepreneurship program, 91% of learners said they would recommend the course to a colleague, and 87% reported being more likely to start their own business after completing the program. Together, these offerings continue to help Uber earners build practical skills that support career advancement, confidence, entrepreneurship, and long-term economic opportunity.

records.Activity Name: Adidas Digital Marketing Series
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): In FY26, ASU and adidas continued to advance global workforce development through the adidas Digital Marketing Series, which now has served over 1,300 learners across five global regions. The program equips wholesale sales teams with digital capabilities to better support retail partners and drive business outcomes. Participants reported an average 46% increase in confidence across key competencies, including conducting digital store checks, acting as trusted advisors, and understanding retailer strategies. ASU also expanded executive and professional skills development through an on-site engagement at adidas U.S. headquarters in Portland, Oregon. Dr. Barbara Lash facilitated a 6.5-hour interactive workshop on public speaking and storytelling for approximately 50 wholesale team members, focused on building clear, confident, and brand-aligned communication. The program received exceptional participant feedback, earning an average likelihood-to-recommend score of 9.7 out of 10 and unanimous positive evaluations.

records.Activity Name: Cigna AI in Action Healthcare Executive Education
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - partner provides funding or sponsorship tied to shared activity outcomes.
records.Activity Description (Short): Through a collaborative business development effort, Instride and ASU secured a new engagement with Cigna for AI. ASU developed a tailored executive education experience based on the university’s AI in Action program, adapted into a half-day format designed for healthcare executives. During FY26, the W. P. Carey School of Business delivered four cohorts, serving 51 leaders across the organization. The program focused on helping executives understand emerging AI opportunities, evaluate strategic applications within healthcare, and identify practical approaches for driving innovation and business impact. Participant feedback was strong, with 79% of learners reporting the content was useful, relevant, and applicable to their role, and 93% indicating the program was engaging and interactive. The success of the initial cohorts positions the partnership for continued growth and expanded engagement opportunities in AI leadership development.

records.Activity Name: Starbucks Career Transition Professional Development Portal
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - source indicates reciprocal or jointly beneficial collaboration.
records.Activity Description (Short): In response to Starbucks’ workforce restructuring efforts, ASU partnered with Starbucks to provide impacted employees with access, at no cost, to a curated portfolio of professional development programs designed to support career transition, reskilling, and career advancement. Through a dedicated learning portal, participants can access learning opportunities in high-demand areas including artificial intelligence, data analytics, leadership, sustainability, and entrepreneurship. The initiative reflects ASU and Starbucks’ longstanding commitment to expanding access to learning and providing practical, career-relevant development opportunities that help individuals navigate periods of professional transition and prepare for future career opportunities.

records.Activity Name: Mayo Clinic-ASU FY26 Strategic Health Innovation Partnership
records.Public Service or Community Engagement: Community Engagement (CE) - formal partnership, reciprocal agreements, jointly developed programs, and shared research/education initiatives indicate collaborative structure and mutual benefit.
records.Activity Description (Short): FY26 accomplishments in the Mayo Clinic-ASU partnership included the launch of the Medical Learning Research Seed Grant Program, reciprocal core facilities agreements, continued development of the Doctor of Nursing Practice Nurse Anesthesia program, the inaugural IMPACT master's cohort, the Health Care Accelerator, and the emerging Engineering Regenerative Therapeutics Seed Grant Program. The work advances medical and health professions education, shared research infrastructure, clinical innovation, workforce development, and commercialization by combining Mayo Clinic's clinical and translational expertise with ASU's strengths in engineering, AI, biomaterials, advanced manufacturing, and education.

records.Mutual Benefits: The partnership created mutual benefit through jointly developed education, research, infrastructure, and innovation programs. ASU and Mayo Clinic launched the Medical Learning Research Seed Grant Program, funded 4 projects, executed reciprocal core facilities agreements allowing researchers at both institutions to access shared research infrastructure at internal rates, advanced the DNP-NA collaboration agreement and accreditation pathway, supported IMPACT students working with Mayo Clinic clinicians and mentors, and developed ERTx as a regenerative therapeutics platform combining Mayo Clinic clinical/translational expertise with ASU engineering, AI, biomaterials, and advanced manufacturing strengths.
